ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-13-2024 Continued To Jul-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gayle Rosenthal and Dr. Robert Myerson appeared via zoom self-represented (petitioners).
Amy Harrington appeared via zoom self-represented (objector).

Ms. Harrington stated that she has a billing email, that she requests administrative fees, and that she has a complete billing record. The Court will issue an order after hearing extending additional time to administer the estate. Status report of administration is granted. Status on close of estate is set for May 13, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Barrett: Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-18-2023 Off Calendar

Petition For Termination Of Proceedings And Discharge
Off Calendar Aug-21-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gayle Rosenthal (co-administrator) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Amy Harrington (former counsel/objector) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Christopher Jew (creditor/interested party) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Robert Meyerson (co-administrator) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mrs. Harrington stated that she objects to the petition being granted. Mr. Jew stated that he has no objections. Mrs. Rosenthal questions what Mrs. Harrington's specific objections are for and stated that if it's an issue of fees, she suggest engaging in settlement. Mrs. Rosenthal also spoke of circumstances regarding Jeffrey's death which occurred in Monterey, Mexico, stated that they filed in San Francisco out of concern about his business (pod cast), and that she is having issues with the fee schedules in California. In addition, Mrs. Rosenthal stated that the estate asset is not a large business, that the pod cast was Jeffrey's personality and with him gone everything has fallen a great deal in revenue, that it is difficult to value the business, that she is willing to entertain any safeguards or conditions that the court would like to put in place, and that she would like to ask for permission to pay the Franchise Tax Board and Mr. Jew's fees. The court stated that a legal basis for termination was not provided, that the probate referee is skilled to put a value on the business and raised the issue of notice/service. Mrs. Harrington stated that she was served with an unfiled document and that she agrees with the court that there is no legal basis for termination of the proceeding. Mrs. Rosenthal asked if it is possible to waive appraisal of the business and go directly to distribution of the business. The court stated that there is no precedent for that. Further discussion was held regarding the taxes and whether there are enough funds to pay said taxes and Mr. Jew's fees. The court stated that the administrator is authorized to pay legitimate creditor's claims. The Petition for Termination of Proceedings and Discharge is denied. The hearing date of September 18th on status on close of estate remains in place.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Petition For Termination Of Proceedings And Discharge
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Continued To Aug-21-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-06-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington, via Zoom; Gayle Rosenthal, via Zoom, and Robert Meyerson, via Zoom, co-Administrators.

The court confirms that neither Administrator opposes Ms. Harrington's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel. The court and the co-Administrators discuss their plan to file a petition to terminate this matter as pro per parties. The court suggests a date in late July and Jul-31-2023 is agreed upon. The court confirms with the co-Administrators to submit a petition for termination which shall be set for hearing on Jul-31-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and instructs them to include in the caption that Judge Moody approved that date at today's hearing.

Ms. Harrington's Motions to be Relieved as Counsel for the co-Administrators are granted by the court subject to review of the orders. Submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.
